---
title: "#clinical"
format: gfm
---


## 2020-09-01

*13:04:39 - @Sean Davis has joined the channel*
**Sean Davis** (13:04:39):
> set the channel description: Discuss clinical applications and standards

*13:05:52 - @Vince Carey has joined the channel*
*13:05:52 - @Levi Waldron has joined the channel*
*13:05:52 - @Leonardo Collado Torres has joined the channel*
*13:05:52 - @Benjamin Haibe-Kains has joined the channel*
*13:05:52 - @Aedin Culhane has joined the channel*
**Sean Davis** (13:05:58):
> [https://github.com/FirelyTeam/RonFHIR](https://github.com/FirelyTeam/RonFHIR)

**Sean Davis** (13:06:29):
> Just wanted to seed a place to discuss clinical and clinical-touching applications.


## 2020-10-21

*13:34:37 - @Kevin Blighe has joined the channel*

## 2020-10-23

*08:17:38 - @Rebecca Howard has joined the channel*
*11:42:35 - @ImranF has joined the channel*

## 2020-11-16

*05:17:42 - @Robert Castelo has joined the channel*

## 2020-11-19

*08:29:24 - @Kevin Blighe has joined the channel*

## 2021-01-22

*15:42:15 - @Annajiat Alim Rasel has joined the channel*

## 2021-02-23

*10:32:26 - @Wynn Cheung has joined the channel*
*10:32:27 - @Wynn Cheung has left the channel*

## 2021-02-24

*11:40:21 - @Kyle Alford has joined the channel*

## 2021-03-20

*01:57:02 - @watanabe_st has joined the channel*

## 2021-05-23

*08:10:01 - @Jonas Schulte-Schrepping has joined the channel*

## 2021-05-28

*10:15:29 - @Izaskun Mallona has joined the channel*

## 2021-08-05

*05:37:34 - @Ambarish S. Ghatpande has joined the channel*

## 2021-08-15

*01:40:44 - @KP has joined the channel*

## 2021-11-08

*03:26:17 - @Paula Nieto García has joined the channel*

## 2021-12-07

*15:20:31 - @Jenny Smith has joined the channel*

## 2022-04-12

*13:59:09 - @Vivian Chu has joined the channel*

## 2022-05-10

**Sean Davis** (14:48:40):
> [https://events.cancer.gov/hdrp/machinelearning](https://events.cancer.gov/hdrp/machinelearning)
  - Attachment (events.cancer.gov): [Machine Learning and Health Outcomes in Cancer Care Delivery Research (Overview) | Events Registration](https://events.cancer.gov/hdrp/machinelearning)
    > Overview The National Cancer Institute is conducting a Machine Learning and Health Outcomes in Cancer Care Delivery Research virtual workshop from May 16-17, 2022 focused on research in machine learning, cancer care delivery, and health outcomes. The goals are to identify scientific gaps, barriers, and opportunities in this emerging area of cancer-focused healthcare research.

*17:58:55 - @Umar Ahmad has joined the channel*

## 2022-07-22

*14:48:15 - @Gary Sieling has joined the channel*

## 2022-07-28

*13:52:05 - @Krithika Bhuvanesh has joined the channel*

## 2022-08-26

*21:04:30 - @Connor Higgins has joined the channel*

## 2022-10-04

**Aedin Culhane** (20:12:03):
> @Sean Davis[https://esciencelab.org.uk/projects/eosc4cancer/](https://esciencelab.org.uk/projects/eosc4cancer/)
  - Attachment (esciencelab.org.uk): [EOSC4Cancer](https://esciencelab.org.uk/projects/eosc4cancer/)
    > Funded by call HORIZON-INFRA-2021-EOSC-01-06 101058427, EOSC4Cancer will provide a federated infrastructure for the exploitation of cancer data for the EU Ca...

**Aedin Culhane** (20:17:05):
  - File (PNG): [image.png](https://community-bioc.slack.com/files/U34P8RS3B/F044YSJDMB8/image.png)

**Aedin Culhane** (20:17:48):
  - File (PNG): [image.png](https://community-bioc.slack.com/files/U34P8RS3B/F0455FDT38A/image.png)

**Aedin Culhane** (20:20:09):
  - File (PNG): [Screenshot 2022-10-05 at 01.19.18.png](https://community-bioc.slack.com/files/U34P8RS3B/F0455FKQ80J/screenshot_2022-10-05_at_01.19.18.png)

**Aedin Culhane** (20:20:22):
> [https://pharmaverse.org](https://pharmaverse.org)
  - Attachment (pharmaverse.org): [pharmaverse](https://pharmaverse.org)
    > This is meta description

**Aedin Culhane** (20:22:45):
> [http://openpharma.github.io](http://openpharma.github.io)


## 2022-10-06

*19:27:39 - @Alan Aw has joined the channel*
*19:27:54 - @kipper fletez-brant has joined the channel*
*19:40:35 - @Amarinder Singh Thind has joined the channel*
*20:45:32 - @Samuel Gamboa has joined the channel*
*20:47:45 - @Spencer Nystrom has joined the channel*
*21:02:34 - @Ana Beatriz Villaseñor Altamirano has joined the channel*
*21:36:55 - @Jonathan Carroll has joined the channel*
*22:03:43 - @Indrik Wijaya has joined the channel*
*23:46:01 - @Marouen Ben Guebila has joined the channel*

## 2022-10-07

*01:25:55 - @Alan Murphy has joined the channel*
*02:50:14 - @Lluís Revilla has joined the channel*
*03:26:36 - @Stephany Orjuela has joined the channel*
*11:22:23 - @Alvaro Sanchez has joined the channel*
**Vince Carey** (17:36:20):
> Here's a very rudimentary exploration of FHIR:[https://vjcitn.github.io/BiocFHIR](https://vjcitn.github.io/BiocFHIR)
  - Attachment (vjcitn.github.io): [Illustration of FHIR ingestion and transformation using R](https://vjcitn.github.io/BiocFHIR)
    > FHIR R4 bundles in JSON format are derived from [https://synthea.mitre.org/downloads](https://synthea.mitre.org/downloads). Transformation inspired by a kaggle notebook published by Dr Alexander Scarlat, [https://www.kaggle.com/code/drscarlat/fhir-starter-parse-healthcare-bundles-into-tables](https://www.kaggle.com/code/drscarlat/fhir-starter-parse-healthcare-bundles-into-tables).

**Vince Carey** (17:38:05):
> Any FHIR experts should not hold back -- this is technically extremely limited.  But I would like it to be a basis for introducing better interrogation and integration technologies in the context of the synthea data and any other public collections.


## 2022-10-08

*17:47:26 - @Taku Iwaki has joined the channel*

## 2022-10-09

*17:19:13 - @Adam Stevens has joined the channel*

## 2022-10-10

*13:55:35 - @Mercilena Benjamin has joined the channel*

## 2022-10-17

**Spencer Nystrom** (10:20:39):
> Not necessarily a Bioc question, but maybe someone here knows. Are DAIDS criteria defined/vendored in a json or other schema somewhere? All I can dig up are the NIAID PDFs with the criteria in plain text....

**Vince Carey** (11:55:19):
> criteria for AIDS or AIDS-related syndromes?  i used to work on HIV clinical trials and know some people at NIAID but what exactly do you need?  what are the pdf links?

**Spencer Nystrom** (16:53:01):
> Ah, sorry. DAIDS grading is criteria for evaluating severity grades of adverse events.[https://rsc.niaid.nih.gov/clinical-research-sites/daids-adverse-event-grading-tables](https://rsc.niaid.nih.gov/clinical-research-sites/daids-adverse-event-grading-tables)For example in this pdf it describes lab value ranges that correspond to different clinical severity levels.[https://rsc.niaid.nih.gov/sites/default/files/daidsgradingcorrectedv21.pdf](https://rsc.niaid.nih.gov/sites/default/files/daidsgradingcorrectedv21.pdf)I was hoping to find a csv file or something that had these criteria to do automated data classifications.


## 2022-10-18

**Vince Carey** (22:21:38):
> @Spencer Nystromthe answer appears to be negative.  I tried to register at the regulatory support center
> but their registration page is messed up for non-NIH users.  If you would like to pursue this further let's discuss in DM.


## 2022-10-19

**Spencer Nystrom** (06:46:09):
> Thanks for giving it a shot, Vince! Really appreciate it.


## 2022-10-24

*10:44:10 - @Filip Stachura has joined the channel*

## 2022-11-17

*17:26:57 - @Konstantinos Geles (Constantinos Yeles) has joined the channel*

## 2023-01-18

*13:09:28 - @José Basílio has joined the channel*

## 2023-01-31

*15:07:38 - @Rebecca Butler has joined the channel*

## 2023-02-28

*15:31:11 - @Ramin has joined the channel*

## 2023-03-15

*01:45:36 - @Amarinder Singh Thind has left the channel*

## 2023-04-28

*16:05:08 - @Vince Carey has left the channel*

## 2023-05-18

*05:54:51 - @Oluwafemi Oyedele has joined the channel*

## 2023-06-19

*05:09:58 - @Pierre-Paul Axisa has joined the channel*

## 2023-08-04

*09:35:04 - @Trisha Timpug has joined the channel*

## 2023-08-08

**Aedin Culhane** (14:31:11):
> Whats the best way to work with  clinVar  and hgvsg in R/Bioconductor?


## 2024-03-26

*16:07:52 - @Sara m.morsy has joined the channel*

## 2024-08-21

*08:56:41 - @Laura Symul has joined the channel*

## 2024-09-05

*03:09:55 - @Mauro Masiero has joined the channel*

## 2024-09-24

*22:48:06 - @Jasmine Baker has joined the channel*

## 2024-10-23

*11:48:30 - @Sounkou Mahamane Toure has joined the channel*

## 2024-12-05

*16:50:56 - @Zahraa W Alsafwani has joined the channel*

## 2025-01-09

*11:35:42 - @Ammar Sabir Cheema has joined the channel*

## 2025-03-18

*14:58:39 - @Nicolo has joined the channel*

## 2025-04-15

**Aedin Culhane** (14:21:54):
> The Italian Biostatistics Group (IBIG) organised a series of 4 webinars focusing on R packages useful for the design and analysis of clinical trials, presented by prominent pharma industry experts:
> 
> - 21 May: Frank Bretz (Novartis) and Dong Xi (Gilead), gMCP: Graph Based Multiple Comparison Procedures.
> 
> - 19 June: Marcel Wolbers and Isaac Gravestock (Roche), rbmi: Reference Based Multiple Imputation.
> 
> - 24 September: Bodo Kirsch (PSI Visualisation Special Interest Group lead), subscreen: Systematic Screening of Study Data for Subgroup Effects.
> 
> - 13 November: Gernot Wassmer and Friedrich Pahlke (RPACT GbR), rpact: Confirmatory Adaptive Clinical Trial Design and Analysis.
> 
> For subscription and further details:[https://simef.it/index.php?option=com_eventbooking&view=event&id=716&catid=7&Itemid=386&lang=it](https://simef.it/index.php?option=com_eventbooking&view=event&id=716&catid=7&Itemid=386&lang=it)
  - Attachment (Società Italiana di Medicina Farmaceutica): [Webinar series on using R software packages](https://simef.it/index.php?option=com_eventbooking&amp;view=event&amp;id=716&amp;catid=7&amp;Itemid=386&amp;lang=it)
    > Webinar series on using R software packages

**Aedin Culhane** (14:22:05):
> Sorry just realized they are charging for these
